highlights:

# ML Platform Engineer
...
Charlotte, NCOn-siteMid-LevelSeniorBackendML / AIDevOps & Infra
...
Build scalable ML and agentic AI platform solutions at a major financial services company, focusing on multi-tenant architecture, MLOps practices, and enterprise-grade infrastructure.
...
Designs, develops, tests, and maintains scalable software, machine learning, and agentic AI platform solutions within a defined technical domain. Builds and supports multi-tenant ML and agentic platforms using established engineering and MLOps practices. Delivers reliable, secure, and high-quality solutions while collaborating with cross-functional teams to execute well-scoped initiatives and enhance platform capabilities.
...
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIESFollowing is a summary of the essential functions for this job. Other duties may be performed, both major and minor, which are not mentioned below. Specific activities may change from time to time.1. Designs, develops, tests, and maintains software components and services that address defined business and user requirements within an assigned technical domain.2. Implements well-scoped features and enhancements using established coding standards, architectural patterns, and development best practices.3. Contributes to the reliability, scalability, and performance of applications by writing high-quality, maintainable code and participating in peer code reviews.4. Collaborates with cross-functional technical teams to execute planned technical initiatives and deliver solutions on schedule.5. Troubleshoots, debugs, and resolves software defects and production issues within area of responsibility, applying root-cause analysis as needed.6. Participates in the full software development lifecycle, including requirements refinement, design discussions, development, testing, deployment, and support.7. Applies secure coding practices, testing strategies, and documentation standards to ensure software quality and compliance with team guidelines.8. Leverages existing frameworks, tools, and technologies to build and enhance applications across relevant technology domains.
...
QualificationsRequired Qualifications
...
1. Bachelor’s degree in computer science, computer engineering, or related field with eight years of experience, or equivalent combination of education and work experience.
...
2. Strong foundation in software engineering, including data structures, algorithms, system design, and enterprise application development, with experience scaling solutions from concept to production.
...
3. Experience designing and developing multi-tenant platforms with capabilities for tenant isolation, governance, scalability, and support for multiple teams and use cases.
...
4. Hands-on experience with ML platform components, including feature stores, model training pipelines, model registry, inference services, and monitoring frameworks.
...
5. Proven experience with MLOps and platform engineering practices, including CI/CD for ML, automated deployment, lifecycle management, and reproducibility.
...
6. Experience building scalable, secure, and cost-efficient cloud-based platforms (e.g., Azure, AWS), including architecture patterns for multi-tenant deployments.
...
7. Experience designing and implementing agentic AI platforms or frameworks, including agent orchestration, multi-agent workflows, tool integration (APIs, retrieval systems), and memory/reasoning patterns using LLMs is a plus.
...
8. Knowledge of governance, security, and responsible AI practices, including data/model governance, regulatory considerations (especially in financial services), and controls for monitoring, auditability, and safe AI adoption is a plus.

### Senior AI Platform Engineer at [Truist](https://www.linkedin.com/company/truistfinancialcorporation) (Current)
...
Architected and delivered AI-native, serverless platform capabilities on AWS, leveraging Lambda, API Gateway, Step Functions, DynamoDB, and EventBridge to enable scalable, real-time financial data processing and intelligent workflow automation. Designed resilient event-driven architectures using SNS, SQS, and DLQ strategies, ensuring reliable message delivery, fault tolerance, and recovery across distributed systems. Built production-grade Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) solutions using AWS Bedrock Knowledge Bases and OpenSearch vector indexes, enabling hybrid search and enterprise AI applications. Developed agentic AI workflows with Claude Code and Cursor to automate code generation, testing, deployment, and developer productivity processes. Integrated Bedrock Agents with guardrails to enforce security, compliance, and responsible AI practices in regulated financial environments. Designed DynamoDB single-table data models with multiple GSIs to support complex access patterns and high-volume transaction datasets. Engineered idempotent, event-driven processing pipelines with Lambda, retry/backoff mechanisms, and orchestration through Step Functions to ensure consistency and reliability across distributed workflows. Implemented end-to-end AI pipelines for document ingestion, embedding generation, retrieval, and inference. Optimized AI inference performance and cost through advanced prompt engineering techniques, including context-window optimization, prompt caching, and model routing strategies. Established CI/CD automation using AWS CodePipeline, GitHub Actions, and AWS CDK for infrastructure-as-code provisioning and deployment standardization.

### AI ML Engineer at [Truist](https://www.linkedin.com/company/truistfinancialcorporation) (Current)
...
Architected enterprise AI platforms across AWS, Azure, and GCP using Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, and multi-cloud networking to support large-scale LLM and automation workloads. Designed cross-cloud LLM strategy leveraging Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, and Vertex AI for distributed inference. Built platform-wide vector search systems using Pinecone, FAISS, Weaviate, and custom embedding pipelines to power enterprise retrieval. Implemented ML lifecycle tracking and observability using MLflow, LangSmith, and internal monitoring systems. Designed multi-agent ecosystems using LangGraph, AutoGen, and intelligent routing engines to orchestrate complex workflows. Developed secure model-serving layers with API Gateway, Nginx, WAF, and IAM, ensuring enterprise-grade access control. Integrated Databricks and Snowflake as unified computation and data platforms for large-scale AI workloads. Automated CI/CD for AI services using GitHub Actions, Jenkins, and container registries, enabling rapid and reliable deployment pipelines. Designed monitoring stacks using Prometheus, Grafana, and OpenTelemetry to capture metrics, logs, and traces across clusters. Built feature store architectures with Feast, Snowflake, and PySpark pipelines to standardize feature access and lineage. Optimized GPU workloads with NVIDIA CUDA, PyTorch enhancements, and TensorRT acceleration. Managed LLM optimization workflows, including quantization, distillation, and efficient transformer variants for performance and cost reduction. Led enterprise AI planning using JIRA roadmaps, Confluence documentation, and architecture review sessions. Documented end-to-end AI architecture using UML diagrams, LucidChart models, and standardized design templates. Results include reducing LLM latency by 40%, improving system uptime to 99.9%, cutting deployment cycles from weeks to hours, improving vector search accuracy by 35%, and lowering cloud costs by 30% through Terraform governance and GPU optimization.

highlights:
Data Engineer AI ML at [Truist](https://www.linkedin.com/company/truistfinancialcorporation)
...
I’m a Data Engineer & AI/ML Practitioner with 4 years of experience building data and machine learning pipelines across banking, healthcare, and research domains. I specialize in designing cloud-native data architectures and AI-integrated workflows that transform raw data into intelligent, production-ready insights. At Truist (Contract), I architected an end-to-end AWS-native credit scoring pipeline, automating encrypted data ingestion and transformation using Lambda, Glue, EMR, and Step Functions. I applied ML techniques such as Hierarchical Clustering, K-Means, Logistic Regression, and Random Forest within SageMaker for entity resolution, credit risk scoring, and anomaly detection—integrated with a Bedrock-based RAG layer for explainable AI insights. Previously at Syracuse University, I built LangChain-powered chatbots and embedding pipelines in Azure Databricks, leveraging MLflow for experiment tracking and deploying models with automated evaluation workflows. Earlier at Accenture, I contributed to HIPAA-compliant AWS data pipelines, optimized ETL performance, and implemented secure, real-time healthcare analytics using Kinesis and Redshift. I’m passionate about scalable data engineering, applied machine learning, and generative AI, and I enjoy turning complex datasets into actionable intelligence using tools like AWS, Azure, Databricks, PySpark, and Terraform. Core Skills: AWS (S3, Glue, EMR, Lambda, Bedrock, SageMaker) · Azure (Databricks, ADF, ML) · PySpark · SQL · Machine Learning (Clustering, Regression, Classification, Anomaly Detection) · Generative AI · RAG · Data Lakehouse · MLOps · CI/CD · Python

...
Architected and implemented an end-to-end AWS-native data pipeline to automate credit score processing from multiple internal and external financial institutions, handling encrypted multi-format data (CSV, JSON, Parquet). Automated data decryption using AWS Lambda + KMS and orchestrated workflows via Step Functions and MWAA (Airflow), reducing manual intervention by 40% and improving audit compliance. Developed PySpark ETL logic on EMR and AWS Glue, cleansing and standardizing 500 GB+ raw credit datasets to a unified schema, improving downstream model accuracy by 30%. Applied ML techniques such as Hierarchical Clustering, K-Means, and Cosine Similarity to detect duplicate and correlated customer entities, integrating the logic with Amazon SageMaker pipelines for automated retraining. Used Logistic Regression and Random Forest classifiers for credit risk categorization and anomaly detection, enhancing fraud flagging and creditworthiness prediction accuracy by 25%. Implemented fuzzy search-and-match logic in Python + SQL within SageMaker endpoints and embedded ML scoring in the ETL flow, achieving 70%+ entity resolution confidence across diverse datasets. Integrated light AI capability using Amazon Bedrock (Titan embeddings + OpenSearch) to build a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) layer that generates explainable insights on customer credit trends and risk behavior. Containerized ML scoring services via Docker and ECS (Fargate) for scalable inference, ensuring consistent deployments across Dev, QA, and Prod. Established CI/CD automation through AWS CodePipeline, CodeBuild, and Terraform, supporting both data and model versioning workflows. results.title: Max Young
highlights:
I am a Master’s in Data Science & Analytics candidate at Georgia State University with experience spanning data analytics, AI evaluation, and applied mathematics. At National DCP, I worked as a Data Analyst supporting supply chain optimization for Dunkin’, where I collected, cleaned, and visualized large datasets to uncover trends from distribution centers to stores. My work with Python, SQL, and Excel enabled actionable insights across logistics operations. I have since expanded into advanced analytics and AI through academic and industry projects with Truist Bank: Graduate Research Assistant & Team Manager – Led a project applying predictive and generative AI to detect mortgage loan fraud, using Python, PySpark, and SQL. Summer Intern – Developed and deployed RAG evaluation pipelines, generating large-scale validation sets and benchmarking frameworks (RAGAS, CLEAR). I engineered retrieval and reranking workflows with LlamaIndex, Qdrant, and Streamlit, improving transparency and reliability of LLMs from providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Amazon Bedrock. Earlier research at Furman University included building a soccer analytics model to rate English Premier League players and creating a web app to visualize RNA sequencing data for biologists. My technical toolkit includes Python, SQL, Apache Spark, Tableau, and modern machine learning methods across regression, clustering, NLP, and AI evaluation. I bring a balance of business-oriented problem solving, strong mathematical foundations, and cutting-edge data science practice. Ultimately, I love making data tell a story. Outside of data, I’m an avid long distance runner, film watcher, and baseball fan—always striving for both performance and perspective.

I build AI that works while you sleep. Then I build the systems that make sure you can trust it. Adaptive learning. Autonomous security. Compliance and risk that watch themselves. Auditable cognitive patterns. The future isn’t AI replacing humans. It’s controlled cognition amplifying what we’re capable of. I’m building that future at Truist.
...
### Head of AI Enablment at [Truist](https://www.linkedin.com/company/truistfinancialcorporation) (Current)
...
I lead the strategy, architecture, and production deployment of autonomous AI systems for enterprise security and the governance infrastructure that keeps AI safe across Truist. I am delivering, digital teammates that do real security work: agents that monitor threat signals continuously, correlate alerts, surface patterns humans would miss, investigate anomalies, and orchestrate response. Autonomous workers operating inside governed boundaries, earning expanded authority as they prove reliability. I am developing enterprise AI security and monitoring: the architecture, controls, and oversight systems that ensure every AI deployment across Truist operates safely, transparently, and within policy. This includes agent observability infrastructure that tracks what AI systems are doing across the enterprise, behavioral monitoring that detects drift, misuse, and anomalous actions, governance frameworks that enforce authorization tiers and audit requirements, and incident response capability when AI systems behave unexpectedly. I am building new teams with positions for engineers, data scientists, and security specialists building these systems from research through production. We will ship. We will measure. We will improve.
...
Hands-on engineering executive and principal architect who designs, builds, and deploys enterprise-grade GenAI and agentic systems in regulated environments. Delivered bespoke agentic solutions for global and local clients across healthcare, government, security, and education, combining retrieval grounding, controlled tool use, hybrid ML inference endpoints, and governance-ready observability. Led distributed teams (20 engineers peak) while shipping production code daily and building repeatable frameworks that accelerate delivery.  My day-to-day involved but was not limited to:  Agentic Systems & GenAI: LLM-enabled applications, agentic workflows (tool use, task decomposition, orchestration), retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), embeddings, vector search, knowledge grounding/source-backed outputs, workflow automation, decision support  Secure AI Engineering (Cybersecurity-Driven): secure-by-design architectures; threat modeling; Zero Trust patterns; RBAC/IAM; secrets management; encryption in transit/at rest; key management; private networking; audit logging; secure SDLC/DevSecOps; governance-ready controls for regulated environments Cloud Platforms: secure AI deployment in Azure, AWS, and Google Cloud; environment separation; policy enforcement; centralized logging/monitoring  Hybrid ML + Gen Systems: ML inference

results.title: Truist patent boom fueled by AI & machine learning
highlights:
- Nearly half (48%) of Truist patent application filings include an AI or machine learning component across technology categories.
- Patent applications are up 63% over the last year.
- Teammate participation is up 58% over the last year.
- Truist reaches Patent 300® list.
...
CHARLOTTE, N.C., June 11, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Truist is accelerating how innovation is developed and deployed across the organization. More teammates are submitting ideas, which helped drive a 63% increase in patent application filings over the past year. These results capture just one part of Truist's innovation story. A closer look at the patent activity shows that nearly half of patent application filings were AI-powered inventions, signaling Truist's deliberate effort to embed machine learning, advanced analytics and automation into core business functions.
...
The growth in patent filings has earned Truist a place on the Patent 300® list, recognizing the top corporate innovators in the United States. The recognition reflects Truist's sustained investment in innovation and its expanding role in advancing award-winning technology that brings its digitally empowered, deeply relational vision to life all while delivering seamless and personalized experiences for clients.
...
"Innovation is defined by how quickly strong ideas can become meaningful outcomes," said Steve Hagerman, Chief Information Officer at Truist and current patent holder. "At Truist, we're creating a faster, more direct path from teammate ideas to real impact for our clients by combining human creativity with the power of AI."
...
This approach is driving measurable increases in participation across the organization. Over the past year, the number of teammates submitting patent applications rose 58%, reflecting a deliberate focus on enabling innovation at all levels of the company and scaling ideas that originate from across the enterprise.
...
Truist's innovation efforts remain grounded in its purpose to inspire and build better lives and communities. As the company expands its use of artificial intelligence, it continues to prioritize responsible development and deployment to ensure innovations deliver meaningful and trusted outcomes for clients.
...
"As AI becomes more deeply integrated into financial services, trust must be built into every solution from the start," said Joy Middleton-Saulny, a Truist AI & Data Executive. "At Truist, we are focused on developing technologies that prioritize security, transparency, and responsible data use, ensuring clients can engage with their finances confidently and securely."
...
Since the inception of its patent program, Truist has secured over 1,200 patents and patent applications. The portfolio includes patents and applications for Truist Client Pulse, an in-house AI innovation that turns millions of client conversations into actionable insights, with traceability to original feedback and continuously refreshed data and models.
...
Truist Financial Corporation is a purpose-driven financial services company committed to inspiring and building better lives and communities. Headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, Truist has leading market share in many of the high-growth markets in the U.S. and offers a wide range of products and services through wholesale and consumer businesses, including consumer and small business banking, commercial and corporate banking, investment banking and capital markets, wealth management, payments, and specialized lending businesses. Truist is a top 10 commercial bank with $549 billion as of March 31, 2026. Truist Bank, Member FDIC. Equal Housing Lender. Learn more at Truist.com.

Currently serving as SVP, Chief Data & Analytics Officer at Truist Financial, where I lead large groups of Ai & Data professionals across Payments, Fraud, AML, Risk, Cyber, and Operations. I hold a Master’s degree in Computer Applications and certifications in AWS, GCP, and ScrumMaster I bring deep expertise in data architecture, cloud computing, data engineering, and business intelligence, designing scalable, high-performance, and cost-effective data products that power self-service analytics and data-driven decision-making. I formulate and execute strategies for data modeling, quality, security, and governance, collaborating across teams and vendors to drive efficiency and innovation

Truist Financial (TFC) is a Financial Services company. Truist Bank offers banking services such as checking and savings accounts, credit cards, mortgages, small business, and commercial banking. Truist Financial employs 18,243 people (+9.5% YoY, +2499 people) and has an annual revenue of $5.9B, founded in 1872. Headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, United States, operates in 25 countries (including India, Philippines, Canada, Georgia, and Panama).
